project.name: skyscanner-app
project.description: A full TypeScript mini-app that searches flights via the Skyscanner RapidAPI, exposes a REST API with /search and /cheapest endpoints, a CLI tool, and an HTML dashboard — built entirely by autonomous multi-agent orchestration

core.description: API client, CLI, and Express server implementation

stories.title: Scaffold TypeScript project with Vitest and Express
stories.description: Create the project skeleton at /tmp/skyscanner-app. Initialize a TypeScript Node.js project with: package.json (name: skyscanner-app, scripts for build/test/start/dev), tsconfig.json (strict mode, target ES2022, module CommonJS, outDir dist/), vitest.config.ts, .gitignore, and a src/ directory. Install runtime dependency express and devDependencies typescript, tsx, vitest, @types/express, @types/node. Do NOT install Skyscanner SDKs — RapidAPI is called directly via fetch. The project must compile cleanly and vitest must exit 0 with no test files present.

specification.coordinatorNotes: Straightforward scaffold story with well-defined ACs and explicit file targets. Needs elaboration on tsconfig module resolution nuances and vitest config details, but no security/edge-case hardening required for a local project skeleton.

agentContributions.notes: Added ACs for .gitignore, src/ directory, and explicit dependency presence in package.json. Tightened tsconfig AC to cover all required compiler options. No split needed — scope is cohesive single scaffold task.

coordinatorReview.reviewNotes: openspec did strong work decomposing compound ACs into atomic, machine-verifiable checks and adding missing structural ACs (.gitignore, src/, explicit dep lists). However, speckit did not run — only one of the two required pipeline agents contributed, which is a process gap that undermines the review objective. Substantive gaps remain: (1) No AC for at least one source file inside src/ — the directory existing is insufficient to confirm a usable scaffold; a src/index.ts stub should be required. (2) vitest.config.ts AC only asserts 'valid TypeScript' with no content constraints — the test include glob pattern and environment (node vs jsdom) should be specified since these affect whether 'vitest run' passes meaningfully. (3) tsconfig AC omits moduleResolution — with module: CommonJS and tsx in dev, the absence of an explicit moduleResolution: node AC is a latent ambiguity that could cause tsc --noEmit failures on path imports. (4) No AC for the Express entry point or a passing smoke-test import — 'npm install completed' + tsc clean is necessary but not sufficient to prove express is wired correctly. The two exit-0 executable ACs are the strongest checks and should be preserved.

stories.title: Implement typed Skyscanner API client
stories.description: Create src/skyscanner/client.ts that wraps the Skyscanner RapidAPI (https://rapidapi.com/skyscanner/api/skyscanner50/). The client should: (1) export a SkyscannerClient class that accepts { apiKey: string } in its constructor, (2) implement searchFlights(params: SearchParams): Promise<FlightResult[]> using Node.js global fetch (Node 18+) to call the Skyscanner API endpoint, (3) export TypeScript interfaces: SearchParams { origin: string; destination: string; date: string; adults?: number }, FlightResult { price: number; currency: string; airline: string; departure: string; arrival: string; duration: number; stops: number }, (4) include proper error handling for HTTP failures and malformed responses, (5) if RAPIDAPI_KEY env var is not set, throw a clear error message. Also create src/skyscanner/client.test.ts with vitest tests that mock fetch and verify: correct URL construction, response parsing, error handling when API returns 4xx, error when no API key. NOTE: Use mock data in tests — do NOT make real API calls in tests.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skyscanner/client.ts exports SkyscannerClient class
SearchParams and FlightResult interfaces are exported
client.test.ts has at least 4 tests all passing
tsc --noEmit exits 0
vitest run exits 0

stories.title: Implement flight search CLI command
stories.description: Create src/cli.ts — a standalone CLI that uses process.argv to accept flight search arguments. Usage: node dist/cli.js --from LHR --to JFK --date 2026-08-01 [--adults 1]. The CLI should: (1) parse arguments using a simple manual parser (no commander, no yargs — keep zero extra deps), (2) require RAPIDAPI_KEY env var and exit with clear error if missing, (3) instantiate SkyscannerClient, call searchFlights, (4) print results as a formatted table to stdout (align columns: airline, price, departure, arrival, duration, stops), (5) if no results, print 'No flights found.' and exit 0, (6) on error, print the error message and exit 1. Also create src/cli.test.ts with vitest tests that mock SkyscannerClient and verify: --from/--to/--date are required, results table is printed, error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: src/cli.ts exists and compiles with tsc --noEmit
CLI prints formatted table when results returned
CLI exits 1 with clear error on API failure
src/cli.test.ts has at least 3 passing tests
vitest run exits 0

stories.title: Build Express REST API with /search and /cheapest endpoints
stories.description: Create src/server.ts — an Express HTTP server that exposes: (1) GET /health → { status: 'ok', uptime: number }, (2) GET /search?from=LHR&to=JFK&date=2026-08-01&adults=1 → { flights: FlightResult[], count: number, searchedAt: string }, (3) GET /cheapest?from=LHR&to=JFK&date=2026-08-01 → the single cheapest FlightResult or 404 JSON if none, (4) GET / → serves src/public/index.html (dashboard). Server reads PORT from env (default 3000), reads RAPIDAPI_KEY from env and passes to SkyscannerClient. Add 'start' script in package.json: 'node dist/server.js'. Also create src/server.test.ts with vitest + supertest (install supertest + @types/supertest) tests verifying: /health returns 200 with status ok, /search returns flights array, /cheapest returns single flight or 404, missing RAPIDAPI_KEY returns 503.
acceptanceCriteria: src/server.ts exports app (Express instance) for testing
/health endpoint returns {status: 'ok', uptime: number}
/search endpoint returns {flights: [], count: number, searchedAt: string}
/cheapest returns the cheapest flight or 404
src/server.test.ts has at least 5 passing tests
tsc --noEmit exits 0
vitest run exits 0

stories.title: Build HTML dashboard at src/public/index.html
stories.description: Create src/public/index.html — a clean, self-contained single-file HTML dashboard (no build step, no npm, just HTML+CSS+vanilla JS). The dashboard should: (1) have a search form with fields: From (IATA code), To (IATA code), Date (date picker), Adults (number, default 1), (2) on form submit, call GET /search?from=...&to=...&date=...&adults=... on the same origin, (3) display results in a styled HTML table with columns: Airline, Price, Departure, Arrival, Duration, Stops, (4) show a 'Find Cheapest' button that calls /cheapest and highlights that row in the results or shows it separately, (5) handle loading state (spinner or 'Searching...') and error state (show error message), (6) dark-mode friendly (use CSS variables, prefers-color-scheme media query), (7) mobile responsive. The file must be entirely self-contained (no external CDN dependencies — inline any CSS/JS needed).
acceptanceCriteria: src/public/index.html exists
Search form has from, to, date, adults fields
Results table renders flight data from /search response
Find Cheapest button calls /cheapest endpoint
Loading and error states are handled
File has no external CDN dependencies
Dark mode and mobile responsive

stories.title: Code review: Skyscanner mini-app
stories.description: Review the full Skyscanner mini-app implementation. Check: TypeScript strict compliance across all src/ files, test coverage (at minimum: client, CLI, server each have tests), security (API key never logged or returned in responses, input validation on query params), error handling completeness, Express route safety (no path traversal for static files). Produce a review document at /tmp/skyscanner-app/review/SKY-006-review.md with findings. The review passes if: tsc --noEmit exits 0, vitest run exits 0, no critical security issues.
acceptanceCriteria: review/SKY-006-review.md written to /tmp/skyscanner-app/review/

vitest run exits 0 in /tmp/skyscanner-app
No API key exposure in source code
All endpoints validated for missing/malformed params
